Details: Removal of Cryptosporidium in water by diatomaceous earth (DE) filtration can be very effective (> 6-log reduction) where the effectiveness is dependent on DE grade permeability and filtration rate. Higher removal rates were observed at higher filtration rates. The removal of Cryptosporidium has been proven to be the most effective due to consistent and proper chemical coagulation and flocculation. A 1997 study found that DE filtration was much more effective at Cryptosporidium removal than conventional and direct granular media filtration. DE filtration is cited in LT1 for its control of Cryptosporidium, assuming proper concurrent turbidity control.
